Additional Info
Success Story
On 01/07/2026 at approximately 1647 hours, Piedmont Police Officers were dispatched to a report of a tool theft in progress. Upon our quick response,
it was determined that unknown suspect(s) had just stolen a 2026 GMC Sierra 3500HD along with an attached dump trailer hauling a large quantity of
concrete. The suspect(s) fled the scene in the stolen vehicle and trailer but lost control several blocks away due to the weight, ultimately colliding with a
residence.
Using nearby surveillance cameras, we observed a suspect vehicle, a dark colored Honda Accord, associated with the theft of the GMC. Through Flock
ALPR technology, I initially captured only a side profile image of the suspect vehicle as it fled out of town. I submitted a request for �additional footage
from one of our Flock cameras and was able to review the footage frame by frame. This allowed me to obtain a partial rear license plate; while four
characters were obstructed, the last three characters were visible.
Using the vehicle description, visible stickers, and the last three characters of the plate, I searched 30 days of history for similar vehicles ending in
�#310. This led to the identification of the suspect vehicles full license plate and a possible suspect believed to be related to or associated with the
registered owner.
The case is still ongoing, but thanks to Flock ALPR technology, we now have strong, reliable leads that are significantly advancing the investigation.
Full ALPR Policy
Piedmont PD's full ALPR policy can be found at the following link:
https://www.ci.piedmont.ca.us/UserFiles/Servers/Server_13659739/File/Government/Departments/Police%20Department/Automated_License_Plate_Readers__ALPRs_%
Disclaimer
The Piedmont Police Department is in the process of transitioning all of its cameras to Flock Safety and so the ALPR data reflected in the Transparency
Portal is not reflective of all the data the Department is collecting.
